stem: A GPS navigation navigation system uses 3 parallel satellite links for redundancy. The position fixed requires at least one satellite link to function. satellite link 1 has a reliability of 87%. satellite link 2 has a reliability between 79% and 91% (uncertain due to limited operational data). satellite link 3 has a reliability of 84%. What is the overall probability that the position fixed?

ground_truth.answer: P(position fixed) ranges from 0.9956 to 0.9981. Point estimate: 0.9969.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 3 satellite links fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.13 × 0.21 × 0.16 = 0.0044. P(works) = 0.9956.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.13 × 0.09 × 0.16 = 0.0019. P(works) = 0.9981.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
